description_long: This is a bright red, vintage-style triplane with three stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft such as the Fokker Dr.I. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the tail, which features a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a visible pilot figure seated inside. The front of the airplane is equipped with a wooden two-blade propeller and exposed engine cylinders, while the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid. The wings are straight and braced with vertical struts, and the overall construction appears to be primarily metal with some wooden elements. The airplane's surface is smooth and painted in a uniform red color, with black and brown accents on the wheels and propeller.
description: A red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller. The design features a streamlined fuselage, exposed engine, and classic taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, open cockpit, and wooden propeller with landing gear visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the fuselage, the stacked wing arrangement, exposed engine cylinders, and tail skid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, single vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the three wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the landing gear and engine details.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, mid-mounted wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with exposed cylinders and a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, mid-fuselage.
